data.title: Spot gold rises above $4,700 for the first time
data.description: Spot gold has broken through $4,700 per ounce for the first time in history, with a gain of 8.8% in the first month of the new year, rising by more than $380. (Golden Ten)

data.title: XRP Faces Bearish Turn After Failed Breakout Attempt
data.description: XRP's recent attempt to break above the $2 mark was unsuccessful, resulting in a swift price reversal. According to NS3.AI, sellers regained control, driving the price downward and causing liquidations of recent long positions. This shift has led to a bearish short-term market structure for XRP.

data.title: Whale Increases Silver Holdings with Leveraged Position
data.description:
On January 20, a prominent investor known for heavy investments in precious metals has increased their leveraged position in silver contracts, according to BlockBeats. The investor, identified by the address 0x271, has established a five-fold leveraged long position in SILVER contracts, totaling 10,000 units valued at approximately $940,000, with an average opening price of $91.3. This position currently shows a floating profit of about $26,000, or 12%.

Previously, due to a short-term dip in silver prices, the investor closed a previous position of 20,000 SILVER contracts, valued at approximately $1.88 million with an average price of $79.6, realizing a profit of around $194,000. The investor continues to focus on long positions in precious metals, with a total contract value of about $5.64 million in gold and silver. Key holdings include:

- PAXG (on-chain gold) long position: approximately $3.05 million, average price $4,429, floating profit around $170,000 (58%).

- GOLD (gold mapping contract) long position: approximately $1.64 million, average price $4,509, floating profit around $64,000 (30%).

- SILVER (silver mapping contract) long position: approximately $940,000, average price $79, floating profit around $27,000 (13%).

- TSLA (Tesla mapping contract): approximately $560,000, average price $465, floating loss around $45,000 (-31%).

The account shows a preference for heavy investments in gold and silver, with an overall leverage of about 5.2 times and a margin usage rate of approximately 80%. The current account balance stands at about $1.435 million, with a return on investment of 68.15% and monthly profits of $380,000.

data.title: G42 Introduces Digital Embassy Framework and Greenshield Sovereign Model
data.description: Abu Dhabi-based artificial intelligence company G42 has announced the launch of a new framework called the 'Digital Embassy' along with a sovereign operational model named Greenshield. According to PANews, this initiative aims to enhance digital sovereignty and operational efficiency. The Digital Embassy framework is designed to provide a secure and efficient digital environment, while Greenshield focuses on establishing a new model for sovereign operations. These developments are part of G42's ongoing efforts to innovate in the field of artificial intelligence and digital infrastructure.

data.title: Researchers suggest that the recent surge in Ethereum activity may be related to address poisoning attacks.
data.description: The recent surge in Ethereum network activity may be related to address poisoning attacks exploiting low gas fees. The week of January 12th saw 2.7 million new addresses added, 170% higher than usual, with over 2.5 million transactions per day. The Fusaka network upgrade in December reduced transaction fees by over 60%, lowering the cost of such large-scale spam attacks. Currently, 3.8 million addresses have received such dust assets in their first transaction, accounting for 67% of the new addresses. To date, 116 victims have lost over $740,000 as a result.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 92,000 USDT with a 1.16% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jan 20, 2026, 05:01 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 92,000 USDT and is now trading at 91,670.5 USDT, with a narrowed 1.16% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Gold Futures Reach Record High of $4,700 Per Ounce
data.description: Gold futures in New York have surged to a historic high of $4,700 per ounce. According to ChainCatcher, this marks a daily increase of 2.28%. Meanwhile, spot gold is currently priced at $4,692.9 per ounce.

data.title: User Loses $3.02 Million in SLVon and XAUt Due to Phishing Signature
data.description: A user has reportedly lost approximately $3.02 million in SLVon and XAUt due to a phishing signature. According to Foresight News, the incident was monitored by Scam Sniffer, highlighting the risks associated with signing fraudulent transactions. This event underscores the importance of vigilance in digital asset management to prevent similar occurrences.

data.title: South Korea indicts three Chinese nationals in connection with a $102 million cryptocurrency money laundering case.
data.description: The Korea Customs and Excise Agency announced the transfer of three Chinese nationals suspected of involvement in a cryptocurrency money laundering scheme to the prosecution. The suspects are accused of transferring approximately 150 billion won (about US$101.7 million) through illegal foreign exchange transactions between September 2021 and June 2023. The agency stated that the suspects used cryptocurrency accounts both domestically and internationally, as well as Korean bank accounts, to transfer funds under the guise of paying for plastic surgery and overseas education. The scheme involved purchasing cryptocurrencies overseas and transferring them to South Korea, then converting them into Korean won and distributing them to local bank accounts to evade regulation. The three individuals have now been transferred to the prosecution for violating South Korea's Foreign Exchange Transaction Act.

data.title: MakinaFi Suffers Major Exploit Resulting in Significant Loss
data.description: MakinaFi, a decentralized finance protocol, has been exploited for 1,299 ETH, equating to approximately $4.13 million. According to NS3.AI, the stolen assets have been transferred to two new cryptocurrency addresses. This incident underscores the persistent security challenges faced by DeFi platforms.

data.title: Chinese Kidnapping Victim Rescued in Pasay City
data.description: Police in Pasay City, Philippines, have successfully rescued a Chinese national who was kidnapped by fellow Chinese individuals demanding a $1 million ransom. According to NS3.AI, the victim was forced to transfer approximately $400,000 in cryptocurrency and pay an additional $14,000 in cash during the ordeal. Authorities have apprehended two suspects, while two others remain at large. The arrested individuals are set to face charges of kidnapping and illegal detention.

data.title: Bitcoin Stabilizes Amid U.S.-Europe Trade Tensions
data.description: Bitcoin has stabilized around $92,000 following a recent sell-off driven by escalating trade tensions between the U.S. and Europe. According to NS3.AI, the market has seen substantial liquidations, yet analysts point to durable ETF inflows as a supportive factor despite short-term volatility. Concerns persist regarding macroeconomic and geopolitical risks, with key uncertainties including the ongoing U.S.-Europe trade dispute over Greenland, delayed regulatory clarity, and pending Supreme Court decisions affecting tariffs.

data.title: Makinafi suffered a loss of approximately 1299 ETH, worth $4.13 million, due to an attack.
data.description: According to PeckShieldAlert monitoring, approximately 1299 ETH, worth about $4.13 million, have been stolen from makinafi. The hack was preempted by MEV Builder (0xa6c2...). The stolen funds are currently held in two addresses: $3.3 million in 0xbed2...dE25 and $880,000 in 0x573d...910e.

data.title: Philippine police rescued a kidnapped Chinese citizen; approximately $400,000 in cryptocurrency was forcibly transferred from the victim's possession.
data.description: Police in Pasay City, Philippines, rescued a Chinese citizen who had been kidnapped by fellow countrymen from an apartment on January 16 and arrested two suspects. Four suspects entered the victim's residence on Thursday and demanded $1 million at knifepoint. During his unlawful detention, the victim was forced to transfer approximately $400,000 to the suspects via cryptocurrency and pay an additional $14,000. Police found duct tape, knives, handcuffs, and a syringe suspected of being used for intimidation at the scene. According to police investigations, one of the suspects had previously been involved in kidnappings for ransom. Two suspects remain at large, while those arrested will face charges of kidnapping and aggravated unlawful detention.

data.title: Coinbase CEO will participate in the drafting of the market structure bill in Davos.
data.description: Coinbase will continue to push forward with the US crypto market structure bill during this week's World Economic Forum and will meet with several bank CEOs to discuss how to achieve a win-win situation. It stated that stablecoins should provide a level playing field for crypto companies and traditional banks and will report the progress of the discussions to the Senate and the government. Previously, Coinbase withdrew its support for the bill because the draft bill proposed prohibiting crypto companies from paying interest on idle stablecoin balances. In addition, Brian Armstrong will also discuss with global leaders the potential of crypto-driven financial system updates and tokenization in capital markets.

data.title: Georgia Orders Crypto Dispensers to Cease Operations Due to Licensing Issues
data.description:
The Georgia Department of Banking and Finance has issued a cease and desist order against Crypto Dispensers, a virtual currency platform operated by Virtual Assets LLC. According to PANews, the platform was found to be operating an online virtual currency exchange and engaging in money or 'monetary value' transmission, including virtual currency, without obtaining the necessary Money Transmitter license or applicable exemption in Georgia.

Under Georgia law, specifically O.C.G.A. § 7-1-681 and § 7-1-681(b), any entity transmitting funds or virtual currency within the United States or internationally must first secure a license or meet exemption criteria. Failure to comply with these requirements is considered illegal.

data.title: Qubits Break Quantum Limit, Extend Information Encoding Fivefold
data.description:
Researchers extended qubit information encoding fivefold by breaking a quantum limit, offering improved stability for computing and precision sensing.
source: https://catenaa.com/industries/technology/qubits-break-quantum-limit-extend-information-encoding-fivefold/

data.title: South Korea Plans to Reform Digital Asset Regulations
data.description:
South Korea is set to reform its digital asset regulatory framework, according to PANews. The country's financial authorities are planning to abolish the "one exchange-one bank" restriction, which will allow the issuance of crypto derivatives and corporate account participation in trading. This move aims to dismantle the current market monopoly structure and enhance liquidity.

Regulators believe that although this restriction is not legally mandated, it has persisted due to anti-money laundering requirements, limiting competition among exchanges and user choice. The upcoming policies will be included in the second phase of the Digital Asset Basic Act legislation. Both political parties in the National Assembly have reached a consensus on some aspects of regulatory relaxation.
